text: The surgeon dissects the lateral mesorectal plane after completing the medial and posterior dissection. During the upper mesorectal dissection, the common iliac vein is visibly injured.
The surgeon quickly compresses the area over the bleeding iliac vein and washes out the blood. A round dissection is performed around the site of the vein injury to manage the bleeding.
A gauze is placed over the iliac vein injury to achieve better compression. The gauze is then removed, revealing the laceration on the vein.

The surgeon applies a flow seal device over the injured common iliac vein, introducing a hemostatic agent to control bleeding. Compression is maintained for three minutes following the application, and a new flow seal application is performed to ensure proper hemostasis.
The surgeon completes the laparoscopic low anterior resection following successful bleeding control, with the surgical field showing no active bleeding.
